title: Conditional Rendering
content:
### v-if, v-else-if, v-else Directives for conditionally rendering elements: ```vue
Type A
Type B
Other
``` Only the matching branch is rendered to the DOM. ### v-show `v-show` toggles the `display` CSS property — the element is always rendered: ```vue
Always in DOM
``` ### v-if vs v-show | Aspect | v-if | v-show | |---|---|---| | Rendering | Lazy (renders only when true) | Eager (always rendered) | | Toggle cost | High (destroys/creates DOM) | Low (toggles display) | | Initial cost | Low if false | High (always renders) | | Use case | Rare toggles, conditional subtrees | Frequent toggles | | v-else support | Yes | No | ### template v-if Use `

quiz.question: What is the key difference between v-if and v-show?
options: v-if removes/adds DOM, v-show toggles display CSS
v-if is faster for frequent toggles
v-if uses CSS, v-show uses DOM

quiz.question: Which HTML element can be used with v-if to toggle multiple elements without adding a DOM node?
